frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing 'INSTRUMENT HANDLING FOR BEGINNERS' with GO PRO and MedSync branding. Two laparoscopic instruments (graspers) displayed on training pad with red surgical lines.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational/instructional content setup — not a performance attempt

frame_analyses.description: Close-up demonstration of grasper instrument handling. Hands in blue nitrile gloves manipulating needle driver and grasper on training pad with pattern lines.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating proper instrument grasp and orientation

frame_analyses.description: Demonstration of scissors handling technique. Two hands holding scissors (left) and grasper (right) above training pad.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational demonstration of instrument control and positioning

frame_analyses.description: Demonstration of grasper closing and opening mechanism on training pad surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Showing proper grasping motion and instrument manipulation

frame_analyses.description: Scissors positioned over training pad showing cutting technique on marked lines.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating precision cutting approach on sample pattern

frame_analyses.description: Training pad showing multiple suture practice lines (straight lines, wavy lines, angles, and V-shapes) with various needle trajectories marked in pen.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Overview of suturing practice patterns and exercise progression

frame_analyses.description: Hands in gloves demonstrating needle holder grasp on training pad with circular pattern outline drawn in pen. Needle driver positioned for suture entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating proper needle holder positioning and entry angle for circular pattern suturing

frame_analyses.description: Two-handed suturing technique shown with both needle driver and grasper positioned on training pad. Demonstrating needle passage and tissue retraction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Two-instrument coordination for suturing — needle driver leading, grasper assisting with tissue control

frame_analyses.description: Continued suturing demonstration on pad with visible suture lines already placed. Demonstrating progression along the pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Sequential suture placement with proper spacing and alignment

frame_analyses.description: Hands positioned above training pad preparing for knot-tying maneuver. Both instruments visible with suture thread visible above the pad.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setting up for extracorporeal or intracorporeal knot tying demonstration

frame_analyses.description: Training pad showing completed suture line with multiple practiced stitches visible. Single suture line with knots tied at intervals.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result showing completed suturing exercise with multiple passes

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of completed suturing practice pad showing final training result with sutures and knots visible along practice lines.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Training exercise completion — all marked lines incorporated into practice

score_components.note: This is instructional content, not a scored performance

confidence_rationale: Video classified as INSTRUCTIONAL. This is a tutorial/educational video with title overlays, MedSync branding, and sequential demonstrations of laparoscopic instrument handling and suturing technique on a training pad. No actual timed performance is shown; content is teaching-focused with narration context implied through visual progression of technique demonstrations.
cannot_determine: Completion time — no single task performance is timed
Specific penalties — this is demonstration, not evaluation
Score — this is educational content, not a candidate performance

demonstrated_techniques: Proper grasper/needle driver grip and positioning
Needle holder orientation for tissue entry
Two-hand coordination (needle driver leading, grasper assisting)
Sequential suture placement with consistent spacing
Suture line progression along marked patterns
verbal_cues: 'GO PRO' branding suggests skill progression framework
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding improper needle driver grasp (demonstrated correct 2/3-point hold)
Preventing shallow or angled tissue entry (demonstrated perpendicular approach)
Maintaining consistent suture spacing and alignment
Proper two-instrument coordination to prevent tissue trauma
teaching_content.notes: This is a beginner-focused instructional video suitable for FLS trainees unfamiliar with laparoscopic instrument handling. Demonstrates foundational techniques before advancing to timed performance tasks.

video_purpose: Educational tutorial for FLS beginners — not a performance evaluation
scoring_recommendation: DO NOT SCORE. This video is instructional content with demonstrations, not a timed trainee or candidate performance. No completion time, no penalties, and no valid FLS score can be assigned.
